comments.value: # Handler gate order (relevant for Part-1 observability):
#   All other handlers:      getAccessSince first, then field validation
# Therefore: empty-ID validation is only directly observable (Validation error) for
# GetThreadMessages — already covered in history-thread.feature. For all other
# handlers the subscription gate fires first, yielding Auth error in Part-1.
# Do NOT duplicate the GetThreadMessages empty-ID or unsubscribed scenarios
#   "if !subscribed { return nil, natsrouter.ErrForbidden("not subscribed to room") }"
#   getAccessSince: ErrForbidden("not subscribed to room"). LoadHistory calls
#   getAccessSince first — subscription gate fires before any Cassandra read.
#   The suite's NATS classifier cannot confirm Auth-class rejection (see
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests history for room "([^"]+)"$
# Category 5 — Subscription required: Load Next Messages
# Source: same as above — getAccessSince called first in LoadNextMessages.
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests next messages in room "([^"]+)"$
# Category 3 + 5 — Invalid cursor (LoadNextMessages) — auth gate fires first
#   called with req.Cursor; ErrBadRequest("invalid pagination cursor") on bad base64.
#   getAccessSince runs BEFORE parsePageRequest, so without a subscription
#   the unsubscribed error fires first. Part-2 can establish a subscription
#   fixture and then verify the cursor-parse branch directly.
#   returns ErrBadRequest("invalid pagination cursor") for bad base64.
#   Auth gate fires before cursor parse in Part-1; documented here so the
#   behavior is catalogued and the cursor-validation branch can be claimed
#   with a subscription fixture in Part-2.
# Category: 3 — Invalid cursor (and 5 — Subscription required, observed in Part-1)
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests next messages in room "([^"]+)" with cursor "([^"]+)"$
# Category 2 + 5 — Missing required field: Load Surrounding Messages (empty messageId)
#   getAccessSince runs BEFORE findMessage; without subscription → Auth error.
#   findMessage: empty messageID → ErrBadRequest("messageId is required").
#   Auth gate fires first in Part-1; empty-ID check is directly observable
#   only once a subscription fixture can be established in Part-2.
# Category: 2 — Missing required field (and 5 — Subscription required in Part-1)
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests surrounding messages with no message id in room "([^"]+)"$
# Category 5 — Subscription required: Load Surrounding Messages (unknown message id)
#   calls getAccessSince → ErrForbidden("not subscribed to room").
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests surrounding messages for unknown message in room "([^"]+)"$
# Category 2 + 5 — Missing required field: Get Message By ID (empty messageId)
#   getAccessSince runs BEFORE findMessage; auth gate fires first in Part-1.
#   Auth gate fires first in Part-1.
# Category: 2 — Missing required field (and 5 — Subscription required in Part-1)
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests message by id "" in room "([^"]+)"$
# Category 5 — Subscription required: Get Message By ID (unknown id)
#   calls getAccessSince → ErrForbidden("not subscribed to room").
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests message by unknown id in room "([^"]+)"$
# Category 2 + 5 — Missing required field: Edit Message (empty messageId)
#   calls findMessage. getAccessSince runs BEFORE findMessage.
#   Auth gate fires first in Part-1.
# Category: 2 — Missing required field (and 5 — Subscription required in Part-1)
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" edits message "" in room "([^"]+)" with new content "([^"]+)"$
# Category 2 + 5 — Missing required field: Edit Message (empty newMsg)
#   TrimSpace(req.NewMsg) == "" → ErrBadRequest("newMsg must not be empty")
#   This runs after the subscription gate AND after findMessage resolves the
#   message. Cannot be directly observed without subscription + real message.
#   Both the subscription gate and the findMessage call run before this check.
#   Part-2 required to exercise the empty-content validation branch.
# Category: 2 — Missing required field (and 5 — Subscription required in Part-1)
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" edits unknown message in room "([^"]+)" with empty content$
# Category 5 — Subscription required: Edit Message (non-empty, unknown id)
#   calls getAccessSince → ErrForbidden("not subscribed to room").
# Category 2 + 5 — Missing required field: Delete Message (empty messageId)
#   calls findMessage. getAccessSince runs BEFORE findMessage.
# Category: 2 — Missing required field (and 5 — Subscription required in Part-1)
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" deletes message "" in room "([^"]+)"$
# Category 5 — Subscription required: Delete Message (unknown id)
#   calls getAccessSince → ErrForbidden("not subscribed to room").
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" deletes unknown message in room "([^"]+)"$
#   getAccessSince runs BEFORE validateThreadFilter; auth gate fires first
#   without a subscription. Part-2 can assert Validation error directly.
#   validateThreadFilter: ErrBadRequest for any value outside "", "all",
#   "following", "unread". getAccessSince runs first; in Part-1 the auth gate
# Category: 4 — Invalid filter (and 5 — Subscription required in Part-1)
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests thread parent messages in room "([^"]+)" with filter "([^"]+)"$
#   calls getAccessSince → ErrForbidden("not subscribed to room").
#   Subscription check runs before filter validation.
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests thread parent messages in room "([^"]+)"$
# Category 6 — Empty result: thread with no replies (Part-2 blindspot)
# This path is only reachable when: (a) caller is subscribed; (b) the parent
# message exists in Cassandra with an empty thread_room_id column. Both
# The Validation step below is a placeholder that exercises the empty-ID check
# (which does fire before the subscription gate for GetThreadMessages) so the
# scenario has a runnable Step; the actual Category-6 behavior is the blindspot.
#   returns NextCursor (base64 PageState) and HasNext=true when more data exists.
#   returns NextCursor (opaque base64 Cassandra PageState) and HasNext=true
#   when more data exists beyond the page limit. A second request using the
#   returned cursor must yield a non-overlapping page.
# Part-2 required: needs Cassandra seed data exceeding the page limit and a
#   valid subscription fixture. In Part-1 the auth gate produces Auth error.
#   § LoadHistory: "GetMessagesBefore returns messages in a room before `before`
# Part-2 required: needs Cassandra fixture with at least two messages at distinct
#   timestamps and a subscription fixture to assert sort order on the response array.

comments.value: # Transport note: message-gatekeeper is reached via a fire-and-forget
# JetStream publish on MESSAGES_<site>; the gatekeeper's reply lands on
# Part-1 has only NATS request/reply primitives — no JetStream publish
# ALL scenarios here are therefore blindspots until Part-2 primitives
# land. They are tagged with the relevant blindspot slug so they fail
# loudly and correctly rather than silently.
# Category abbreviations used in comments below:
#   CAT-8  bad room subject (user not subscribed)
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" submits a valid message to room "([^"]+)"$
# NEW STEP: ^the response is an accepted reply$
# NEW STEP: ^within ([0-9]+)s a canonical created event exists for the message in room "([^"]+)"$
#       return nil, fmt.Errorf("content exceeds maximum size of %d bytes", maxContentBytes)
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" submits a message with content larger than 20 KB to room "([^"]+)"$
# CAT-5: Bad thread parent fields — missing createdAt companion
#       return nil, fmt.Errorf("validate thread parent fields: threadParentMessageCreatedAt
#           is required when threadParentMessageId is set")
# Category: CAT-5 — bad thread parent fields
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" submits a thread reply to room "([^"]+)" with threadParentMessageId set but no threadParentMessageCreatedAt$
# CAT-5: Bad thread parent fields — invalid thread parent message ID format
#       return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid thread parent message ID %q: must be a 20-char base62 string",
# Category: CAT-5 — bad thread parent fields
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" submits a thread reply to room "([^"]+)" with a malformed threadParentMessageId$
# CAT-5: Bad thread parent fields — cross-thread quote context mismatch
#       return nil, fmt.Errorf("quoted parent %s thread context mismatch: ...")
# Category: CAT-5 — bad thread parent fields (cross-thread quote)
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" submits a message to room "([^"]+)" quoting a message from a different thread$
# CAT-7: Bad quote — main-room message quoting a thread reply
#   § "Scope": "Main-room messages can only quote main-room messages;
#     messages inside thread T can only quote other messages in thread T."
#   The snap.ThreadParentID check enforces the same-context rule.
# Category: CAT-7 — bad quote / mention list
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" submits a main-room message to room "([^"]+)" quoting a thread reply$
# CAT-7: Bad quote — referenced parent message not found
#     "Client sends invalid UUID for quotedParentMessageId → RPC returns
#      NotFound → request fails, error replied to client"
#     "Parent message not found in Cassandra → RPC returns NotFound →
#      request fails, error replied to client"
# Category: CAT-7 — bad quote list (non-existent parent)
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" submits a message to room "([^"]+)" quoting a non-existent message id$
# CAT-8: Bad room subject — user not subscribed
#     "User not in room → Reply error, ack message"
#       return nil, fmt.Errorf("user %s is not subscribed to room %s", account, roomID)
# Category: CAT-8 — bad room subject (not subscribed)
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" submits a message to room "([^"]+)" on behalf of a non-member$
# CAT-8 / Large-room restriction — non-privileged user blocked in large room
#   "in rooms with more than the configured threshold of members, only
#    owners, admins, and bots may send top-level messages. Thread replies
#    are exempt regardless of room size."
# Category: CAT-8 — large-room post restriction (Part-2: requires Mongo seeding)
# NEW STEP: ^a channel room "([^"]+)" exists with more than ([0-9]+) members$
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" is a regular member of room "([^"]+)"$
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" submits a top-level message to room "([^"]+)"$
# CAT-8 / Large-room restriction — thread reply exempt from large-room cap
# Category: CAT-8 — large-room restriction exemption for thread replies
# (reuses NEW STEPs declared in previous scenario)
#   § "5. Data Flow & Error Handling": subscription check → ErrForbidden
# NOTE: Edit lives in history-service, not message-gatekeeper; the
# This is a NATS request/reply and IS observable in Part 1 via natsRequest.
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to edit message "([^"]+)" in room "([^"]+)" with new content "([^"]+)"$
# CAT-9: Edit-message validation — empty newMsg rejected (history-service)
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to edit message "([^"]+)" in room "([^"]+)" with empty content$
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to delete message "([^"]+)" in room "([^"]+)"$
# Extra category: CAT-3 / siteID mismatch ack-and-drop

# NOTE: a siteID-mismatched message is silently acked with no client reply
# (no RequestID reply is sent for this code path). The "no reply" contract

comments.value: # message-worker has NO synchronous reply path observable in Part 1.
# Its entire observable output is state-mutation in Cassandra (and MongoDB
# for thread rooms/subscriptions) plus JetStream publishes to OUTBOX.
# All scenarios below require Part-2 primitives (Cassandra query observation,
# JetStream stream-peek, or MongoDB observation) and are therefore tagged
# @blindspot. They are drafted now to auto-resolve when Part 2 lands.
# Category 1: Persistence post-condition (canonical create → Cassandra rows)
#         both messages_by_room and messages_by_id with room_id, bucket, created_at,
#         message_id, sender, msg, site_id, updated_at, mentions, type, sys_msg_data,
# Trigger: a valid MessageEvent is published to chat.msg.canonical.<site>.created.
# Post-condition (Part-2 Cassandra observe): the message row exists in
#   messages_by_room and messages_by_id with the expected columns populated.
# Source: docs/cassandra_message_model.md § messages_by_room PRIMARY KEY((room_id, bucket),
# Source: CLAUDE.md § "Bucketed message tables": MESSAGE_BUCKET_HOURS must match
#         across all services; mismatch silently loses data.
# Post-condition: the bucket column in the persisted row equals
# Category 3: Thread row writes (thread replies → thread_messages_by_room)
#         messages_by_id and thread_messages_by_room, then calls incrementParentTcount
#         (LWT CAS on both messages_by_id and messages_by_room of the parent row).
# Post-conditions: row in thread_messages_by_room; parent's tcount incremented by 1
# Category 3b: ThreadRoom and ThreadSubscription written to MongoDB
#         for the parent author and the replier (if distinct).
# Category 4: Edit propagation (canonical .updated → NOT handled by message-worker)
#         § D4: message-worker's consumer FilterSubject is set to
# Post-condition (JetStream-peek): a canonical .updated event published to
#   MESSAGES_CANONICAL is not consumed by message-worker's durable consumer.
#   No new row appears in Cassandra for the edit event alone.
# Category 5: Delete propagation (canonical .deleted → NOT handled by message-worker)
#         § D4 and § 3.4: message-worker stays on .created only; history-service owns
# Post-condition (JetStream-peek): a canonical .deleted event published to
#   MESSAGES_CANONICAL is not delivered to message-worker's durable consumer.
#         is idempotent on its primary key — the PRIMARY KEY of messages_by_room is
#         ((room_id, bucket), created_at, message_id) and messages_by_id is
#         (message_id, created_at), so a duplicate publish writes the same row.
#         § Error Handling: "Cassandra insert failure → NAK; idempotent retry acceptable
#         since message_id is part of primary key."
# Post-condition: after two deliveries of the same event, exactly one row exists
#   (Cassandra upsert semantics on primary key collision).
# Category 7: Order independence — distinct messages across buckets
#         § Bucket scheme: bucket is derived deterministically from created_at, not from
#         delivery order. Each message lands in its own bucket regardless of JetStream
# Source: CLAUDE.md § "Bucketed message tables": all services that read or write
#         messages_by_room must use the same MESSAGE_BUCKET_HOURS.
# Post-condition: each message lands in its own correct bucket partition independently.
# Category 8: Handler error reporting — Cassandra unavailable → NAK
# Source: message-worker/handler.go § HandleJetStreamMsg: if processMessage returns
#         an error, msg.Nak() is called so JetStream redelivers the message.
#         The code logs slog.Error("process message failed") and slog.Error("failed to nack
#         fmt.Errorf("save message %s: %w", msg.ID, err) on ExecuteBatch failure.
# Post-condition (JetStream-peek): the canonical event is NOT acked; its delivery count
#   increases (JetStream redelivery semantics). Verifying the NAK is a Part-2 primitive
# Category 8b: Cross-site thread subscription outbox published for remote replier
#         § Outbox routing: one outbox event per (reply, affected user) when the user's
#         home site differs from the room's home site.
# Category: Handler error reporting / cross-site federation observable side-effect
# Post-condition (JetStream-peek on OUTBOX): an outbox event of type

comments.value: # room-worker is an async JetStream consumer. The client interacts with
# room-service over NATS request/reply; room-service validates and replies
# synchronously with {"status":"accepted"} (or an error). The canonical
# event is then published to the ROOMS_{siteID} JetStream stream; room-worker
# consumes it and publishes an AsyncJobResult to
# The synchronous room-service acceptance/rejection reply is observable in
# Part-1 via NATS request/reply. The room-worker's downstream actions
# (Mongo writes, outbox events, async AsyncJobResult on the response subject)
#   (a) a pre-armed NATS subscription on the response subject before the
#       JetStream message is published — the Part-2 "JetStream + decoupled
#   (b) Mongo / JetStream observation steps — also Part-2.
# These downstream contracts are tagged @blindspot until those primitives
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" is the owner of channel room "([^"]+)"$
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to add member "([^"]+)" to room "([^"]+)"$
# Note: room-service forwards the X-Request-ID header; the test exercises
# what happens when no request ID is present in the NATS headers. In
# practice the suite always sends a request ID via the natsRequest helper
# (it sets X-Request-ID in the NATS header); this scenario is therefore a
# blindspot until the suite can suppress the header.
# Category: Validation rejection — add to non-channel room
#   "Room type guard: If room.Type != RoomTypeChannel, reject with error
#    "cannot add members to a DM room""
# Note: room-service enforces this before publishing; the error arrives in
# the synchronous reply from room-service, not from room-worker.
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to add member "([^"]+)" to DM room between "([^"]+)" and "([^"]+)"$
# Category: Authorization rejection — restricted room, non-owner requester
#   "Restricted room guard: If room.Restricted == true and requester does not
#    have owner role, reject with error "only owners can add members to this room""
# Category: State precondition — room does not exist
#   "GetSubscription(requester, roomID) confirms requester is in the room"
# A non-existent room means no subscription, so this fails the subscription
# check before even reaching the room-type guard.
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to add member "([^"]+)" to room "([^"]+)" that does not exist$
# Category: Persistence post-condition (blindspot until Part-2 Mongo observation)
#   "BulkCreateSubscriptions — For each user, create Subscription with
# Category: Outbox propagation (blindspot until Part-2 JetStream observation)
#   "Outbox for cross-site members (batched by destination site) — publish to
#   "On success, publish the validated request to subject.RoomCanonical
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to leave room "([^"]+)"$
# Category: Authorization rejection — non-owner cannot remove another member
#   "Owner authorization: reject if the requester's Roles do not contain 'owner'"
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to remove member "([^"]+)" from room "([^"]+)"$
#   "Last-owner guard: reject if target has the 'owner' role AND ownerCount <= 1"
# Category: Persistence post-condition (blindspot until Part-2 Mongo observation)
# Category: Happy-path reply — promote to owner
#   "On success: marshal the request, publish to the ROOMS stream,
# Category: Happy-path reply — demote from owner
# Category: Authorization rejection — non-owner cannot update roles
#   "Requester must have a subscription with HasRole(sub.Roles, RoleOwner):
# Category: State precondition — last-owner self-demote guard
#    "cannot demote: you are the last owner""
# Category: Persistence post-condition (blindspot until Part-2 Mongo observation)
# Category: Outbox propagation (blindspot until Part-2 JetStream observation)
#   "If user.SiteID != h.siteID: publish OutboxEvent with type "role_updated"

name: Room service — member operations and supporting RPCs

comments.value: # Scope: service (single request/reply, no cross-service pipeline)
# This file covers handlers NOT already exercised by room.feature
# CREATE ROOM — validation variants (DM / edge cases)
# Avoids duplicating the channel-happy-path in room.feature
#   errSelfDM = errors.New("cannot create a DM with yourself")
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to create a DM with user "([^"]+)"$
#   "missing X-Request-ID header" is returned before any DB call when the
#   request context carries no request ID from headers.
# Note: the integration harness (natsRequest_test.go) sets traceparent but
# This behavior is observable without infrastructure (no room seeding needed).
#   errChannelNameTooLong = errors.New("channel name must be at most 100 characters")
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to create a channel room with a name of (\d+) runes$
#   errEmptyCreateRequest = errors.New("request must include at least one of users, orgs, channels, or name")
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to create an empty room$
#   errBotInChannel = errors.New("bots cannot be added to a channel")
#   isBot() matches ".bot" suffix or "p_" prefix accounts
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to create a channel room "([^"]+)" with bot user "([^"]+)"$
#   natsCreateRoom special-cases dmExistsError: replies {"error":"dm already exists","roomId":"<id>"}
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to create a DM with user "([^"]+)"$
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to add member "([^"]+)" to room "([^"]+)"$
#   "only owners can add members to a restricted room"
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to add member "([^"]+)" to room "([^"]+)"$
#   "cannot add members to a non-channel room"
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to add member "([^"]+)" to room "([^"]+)"$
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" has created a DM room with "([^"]+)"$
#   "Reject direct bots up front — a client that explicitly lists a bot
#    must see a hard error rather than a silent drop."
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to remove member "([^"]+)" from room "([^"]+)"$
# This scenario requires org membership seeding (Part 2 Mongo primitive).
# Marking as blindspot: the end-to-end observable behavior depends on
# org-member state that cannot be set up via the current suite primitives.
# Source: room-service/helper.go — fmt.Errorf("last owner cannot leave the room")
#   § "Validation Rules" step 3: exactly one of account or orgId
#   "exactly one of account or orgId must be set"
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to remove a member from room "([^"]+)" with no account or orgId$
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to list members of room "([^"]+)"$
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests to list members of room "([^"]+)" with limit (\d+)$
#   § 2.3 Validation: "len(RoomIDs) == 0 → roomIds must not be empty"
# NEW STEP: ^the room info batch RPC is called with (\d+) room IDs$
#   § 2.3 Validation: "len(RoomIDs) > MAX_BATCH_SIZE → batch size N exceeds limit M"
#   § 2.3 Response semantics: Found=false entry for a missing room ID
# NEW STEP: ^the room info batch RPC is called with room ID "([^"]+)"$
# NEW STEP: ^the response contains a room info entry with "([^"]+)" set to "([^"]+)"$
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests read receipts for message "([^"]+)" in room "([^"]+)"$
# NEW STEP: ^"([^"]+)" requests read receipts with no message ID in room "([^"]+)"$
#   "only the message sender can view read receipts"
# The suite lacks a primitive to create a real message and confirm its
# message ID end-to-end (requires JetStream + message-worker pipeline,
# Part 2). Marking as blindspot: the non-sender guard path requires
# a seeded message with a known sender.
# TODO: add-member capacity exceeded (MAX_ROOM_SIZE) — needs a room pre-seeded
#       with MAX_ROOM_SIZE-1 members, then one more add; requires Part 2
#       Source: room-service/handler.go::handleAddMembers — "room is at maximum capacity"
# TODO: create channel via channel-ref expansion — requester not a member of
# TODO: list members with enrich=true returns display fields (engName, isOwner).
#       Requires Part 2 Mongo observation to verify enriched fields.
# TODO: remove-member exactly one of account/orgId both set is rejected.
#       Source: room-service/handler.go::handleRemoveMember — "exactly one of account or orgId must be set"
#       Add a step that sends both fields.
# TODO: role-update org-only target cannot be promoted to owner.
#       establish org-only membership before the promote request.
# TODO: rooms info batch — happy path with a known room returns Found=true and
#       correct name. Requires either a pre-seeded room or a create-then-batch
